title: How to Pray the Rosary
introduction: The Rosary is the most powerful prayer after the Holy Sacrifice of the Mass. Our Lady herself asked for its daily recitation at Fatima. This guide will walk you through every step. You will need a rosary — a set of beads with a crucifix, available at any Catholic bookstore or parish gift shop.

steps.title: Hold the Crucifix
steps.description: Begin by holding the crucifix in your hand. Make the Sign of the Cross: 'In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost. Amen.' Kiss the crucifix. You are beginning a conversation with God through His Mother.

steps.title: The Apostles' Creed
steps.description: Still holding the crucifix, recite the Apostles' Creed. This is a profession of faith — you are declaring what you believe before you begin to pray. It is the same Creed recited at Baptism.

steps.title: First Our Father
steps.description: Move to the first large bead after the crucifix. Pray the Our Father (Pater Noster). This Our Father is offered for the intentions of the Holy Father, the Pope.

steps.title: Three Hail Marys
steps.description: Move to the three small beads. Pray one Hail Mary on each bead. These three Hail Marys are offered for an increase in the theological virtues: the first for Faith, the second for Hope, and the third for Charity.
steps.note: First for Faith, second for Hope, third for Charity

steps.title: Glory Be
steps.description: After the three Hail Marys, pray the Glory Be (Gloria Patri). This short prayer of praise to the Holy Trinity concludes the introductory prayers.

steps.title: Announce the First Mystery
steps.description: Before each decade, announce the mystery to be meditated upon. For example: 'The First Joyful Mystery: The Annunciation.' Then pause briefly to call the scene to mind. The mysteries are the heart of the Rosary — the beads are a tool to keep your place while your mind contemplates the life of Christ.
steps.note: The mysteries change by day. The app will guide you through the correct set.

steps.title: Our Father (Decade)
steps.description: On the large bead, pray the Our Father. This begins the decade. Each decade consists of one Our Father, ten Hail Marys, one Glory Be, and the Fatima Prayer.

steps.title: Ten Hail Marys
steps.description: Move through the ten small beads, praying one Hail Mary on each. While your lips say the words, your mind meditates on the mystery. Do not rush. Let the repetition become a rhythm that frees your mind to contemplate. If you lose your place, do not worry — simply continue. Our Lady does not count your beads; she counts your love.

steps.title: Glory Be & Fatima Prayer
steps.description: After the ten Hail Marys, pray the Glory Be, then the Fatima Prayer ('O my Jesus, forgive us our sins...'). The Fatima Prayer was requested by Our Lady in 1917 and is prayed after each decade.

steps.title: Repeat for Five Decades
steps.description: Repeat steps 6 through 9 for each of the five mysteries. Announce the mystery, pray the Our Father on the large bead, ten Hail Marys on the small beads, and the Glory Be and Fatima Prayer. When you complete the fifth decade, you will have prayed 53 Hail Marys, 6 Our Fathers, and meditated on 5 mysteries.

steps.title: Hail Holy Queen
steps.description: After the fifth decade, pray the Hail Holy Queen (Salve Regina). This great Marian prayer closes the Rosary with a final appeal to Our Lady's intercession.

steps.title: Final Prayer & Sign of the Cross
steps.description: Conclude with the prayer 'O God, whose only-begotten Son...' or simply make the Sign of the Cross. Some add the Sub Tuum Praesidium or the Prayer to St. Michael. Kiss the crucifix. The Rosary is complete. You have just prayed the most powerful prayer a layperson can offer.

mysterySchedule.saturdayNote: Some traditional sources assign the Glorious Mysteries to Saturday. Both are acceptable.

tips: Start with one decade a day if five feels overwhelming. One decade prayed well is better than five decades prayed distractedly.
Pray at the same time each day to build a habit. Many families pray the Rosary together after dinner.
If your mind wanders, gently bring it back to the mystery. This is normal and not a sin. The effort itself is meritorious.
A physical rosary helps enormously. The tactile sensation of the beads keeps your hands occupied and helps maintain focus.
The Rosary can be prayed anywhere — in the car, while walking, waiting in line. You do not need to kneel or be in a church.
Offer each Rosary for a specific intention: a loved one, the conversion of a sinner, the Holy Souls in Purgatory, or world peace.
